title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: onet_archive_read() reads O*NET text archive table normalizes one panel schema. local path argument useful downloaded archives manually want reproducible, offline workflow. output one row per occupation, element, scale, release. important longitudinal fields : release_version release_date, identify archive release; soc_vintage, onet_soc_code, soc_code, identify taxonomy; data_value, descriptor score; source_date domain_source, help distinguish fresh updates carryforwards method changes.

text: example releases use 2019 O*NET-SOC vintage, crosswalk needed. Calling onet_crosswalk_bridge("2019", "2019") returns empty bridge schema onet_panel_reconcile() can use -vintage comparisons. real cross-vintage work, use onet_crosswalk_bridge() compose official adjacent O*NET-SOC crosswalks.

title: Reproducible user measures
text: Task Statements carries task ids Core Supplemental labels. Task Ratings carries relevance, importance, frequency ratings used aggregate task-level scores.
code: archive_dir <- system.file(   "extdata",   "onet-mini",   "db_30_3_text",   package = "onet2r" ) source_archive_dir <- file.path("inst", "extdata", "onet-mini", "db_30_3_text") if (   !file.exists(file.path(archive_dir, "task_statements.txt")) &&     dir.exists(source_archive_dir) ) {   archive_dir <- source_archive_dir }  tasks <- onet_archive_read(   "30.3",   "Task Statements",   path = archive_dir,   release_date = "2026-05-01" ) task_ratings <- onet_archive_read(   "30.3",   "Task Ratings",   path = archive_dir,   release_date = "2026-05-01" )  tasks |>   select(onet_soc_code, task_id, task_type, task) |>   head() |>   print(width = Inf) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   onet_soc_code task_id task_type    #>   <chr>         <chr>   <chr>        #> 1 15-1252.00    1001    Core         #> 2 15-1252.00    1002    Supplemental #> 3 29-1141.00    2001    Core         #>   task                                          #>   <chr>                                         #> 1 Analyze user needs and software requirements. #> 2 Prepare reports on software testing status.   #> 3 Monitor patient health and record signs. task_ratings |>   select(onet_soc_code, task_id, scale_id, data_value, recommend_suppress) |>   head() |>   print(width = Inf) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   onet_soc_code task_id scale_id data_value recommend_suppress #>   <chr>         <chr>   <fct>         <dbl> <chr>              #> 1 15-1252.00    1001    RT             95   N                  #> 2 15-1252.00    1001    IM              4.5 N                  #> 3 15-1252.00    1002    RT             45   N                  #> 4 29-1141.00    2001    RT             98   N                  #> 5 29-1141.00    2001    IM              4.8 N

title: Reproducible user measures

code: task_scores <- tibble::tibble(   task_id = c("1001", "1002", "2001"),   score = c(0.80, 0.40, 0.20) )  measure <- onet_measure(   task_scores,   key = "task_id",   score = "score",   key_type = "task",   universe = tasks$task_id,   measure_id = "stylized_task_score",   measure_name = "Stylized task score",   release_version = "30.3" )  onet_measure_coverage(measure) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   key_type n_input n_universe n_matched coverage_share employment_coverage_share #>   <chr>      <int>      <int>     <int>          <dbl>                     <dbl> #> 1 task           3          3         3              1                        NA if (nrow(measure$unmatched) == 0) {   tibble::tibble(status = "No unmatched task ids") } else {   measure$unmatched } #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   status                #>   <chr>                 #> 1 No unmatched task ids

what: Join OEWS to O*NET Occupation Codes
title: Adding OEWS Wage and Employment Context
text: O*NET-SOC codes commonly include detail suffix .00. OEWS uses six-digit SOC codes. onet_join_oews() creates normalized soc_code joins value. join also tells wage context missing. Missing rows usually mean need different OEWS geography, different year, crosswalk classification vintages.
code: occupation_wages <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews)  occupation_wages |>   select(code, title, soc_code, tot_emp, a_median, h_median) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   code       title                            soc_code tot_emp a_median h_median #>   <chr>      <chr>                            <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00 Software Developers              15-1252  1847900   133080     64.0 #> 2 29-1141.00 Registered Nurses                29-1141  3175400    93070     44.8 #> 3 11-1011.00 Chief Executives                 11-1011   211230   206680     99.4 #> 4 41-1011.00 First-Line Supervisors of Retai… 41-1011       NA       NA     NA occupation_wages |>   transmute(     code,     title,     has_oews_match = !is.na(tot_emp)   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   code       title                                          has_oews_match #>   <chr>      <chr>                                          <lgl>          #> 1 15-1252.00 Software Developers                            TRUE           #> 2 29-1141.00 Registered Nurses                              TRUE           #> 3 11-1011.00 Chief Executives                               TRUE           #> 4 41-1011.00 First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers FALSE

title: Adding OEWS Wage and Employment Context
text: average ability scores across occupations without weights, niche occupation counts occupation employing millions workers. onet_weighted_summary() uses OEWS employment, optionally wage, summarize O*NET descriptor rows labor-market level. example, n_occupations number O*NET occupations matching OEWS estimate ability row. total_weight summed OEWS employment used weighted mean.
code: weighted_abilities <- onet_weighted_summary(   abilities,   group = c("element_id", "element_name"),   value = "data_value",   occupation_code = "onet_soc_code",   oews = oews )  weighted_abilities |>   print(width = Inf) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   element_id element_name        n_records n_occupations total_weight #>   <chr>      <chr>                   <int>         <int>        <dbl> #> 1 1.A.1.a.1  Oral Comprehension          3             3      5234530 #> 2 1.A.1.b.1  Problem Sensitivity         3             3      5234530 #>   weighted_mean wage_weighted_mean #>           <dbl>              <dbl> #> 1          4.57               4.54 #> 2          4.73               4.68

title: Within-versus-between decomposition
text: first occupation safe within-change attribution. second , within movement goes unclassifiable bucket. component rows total_change sum total change.
code: from_scores <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   measure_score = c(1.0, 2.0),   safely_comparable = c(TRUE, FALSE) ) to_scores <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   measure_score = c(2.0, 2.5),   safely_comparable = c(TRUE, FALSE) ) from_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   employment = c(100, 100) ) to_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   employment = c(150, 50) )  from_scores #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   reference_soc_code measure_score safely_comparable #>   <chr>                      <dbl> <lgl>             #> 1 15-1252                        1 TRUE              #> 2 29-1141                        2 FALSE to_scores #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   reference_soc_code measure_score safely_comparable #>   <chr>                      <dbl> <lgl>             #> 1 15-1252                      2   TRUE              #> 2 29-1141                      2.5 FALSE decomp <- onet_decompose_change(   from_scores,   to_scores,   from_weights,   to_weights )  decomp #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   component       value #>   <chr>           <dbl> #> 1 within          0.5   #> 2 between        -0.25  #> 3 interaction     0.125 #> 4 unclassifiable  0.25  #> 5 total_change    0.625 attr(decomp, "coverage") #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   n_common n_safely_comparable leakage #>      <int>               <int>   <dbl> #> 1        2                   1       0 decomp |>   summarise(     component_sum = sum(value[component != "total_change"]),     total_change = value[component == "total_change"],     difference = component_sum - total_change   ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   component_sum total_change difference #>           <dbl>        <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1         0.625        0.625          0

title: Within-versus-between decomposition
text: decomposition can run users prepare weight panels demographic cells. package download raw PUMS data examples, fixture follows reference-SOC, year, cell, employment shape. cell weight shift unclassifiable bucket. part reviewers need see aggregate result depends vintage bridges, source dates, task-handling choices.
code: from_weights_by_sex <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141", "15-1252", "29-1141"),   sex = c("F", "F", "M", "M"),   employment = c(40, 70, 60, 30) ) to_weights_by_sex <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141", "15-1252", "29-1141"),   sex = c("F", "F", "M", "M"),   employment = c(70, 50, 80, 0) )  split_results <- lapply(unique(from_weights_by_sex$sex), function(cell) {   result <- onet_decompose_change(     from_scores,     to_scores,     from_weights_by_sex |> filter(sex == cell),     to_weights_by_sex |> filter(sex == cell)   )   result$sex <- cell   result }) |>   purrr::list_rbind()  split_results |>   print(n = Inf, width = Inf) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    component       value sex   #>    <chr>           <dbl> <chr> #>  1 within          0.364 F     #>  2 between        -0.220 F     #>  3 interaction     0.110 F     #>  4 unclassifiable  0.318 F     #>  5 total_change    0.572 F     #>  6 within          0.667 M     #>  7 between        -0.333 M     #>  8 interaction     0.167 M     #>  9 unclassifiable  0.167 M     #> 10 total_change    0.667 M

title: Access O*NET Web Services API data from R
text: Current O*NET API data: onet_search(), onet_occupation(), onet_skills(), onet_tasks(), onet_table(). Archived O*NET data: onet_releases(), onet_archive_download(), onet_archive_read(), onet_panel(), onet_panel_reconcile(). Wage employment context: onet_oews_national(), onet_join_oews(), onet_weighted_summary(), onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums(). User-measure plumbing: onet_measure(), onet_task_to_occupation(), onet_measure_aggregate(), onet_robustness_diagnostic(), onet_decompose_change().

what: onet2r: Access 'O*NET' Web Services API — onet2r-package
title: onet2r: Access 'O*NET' Web Services API — onet2r-package
text: Access 'O*NET' Web Services Application Programming Interface (API) version 2.0. Search occupations, retrieve occupation details skills, knowledge, abilities, tasks, work activities, access database tables, perform taxonomy crosswalks, join wage employment context, assemble longitudinal archive panels, validate user-supplied occupational measures. Results returned tibbles easy integration tidyverse workflows.

title: onet2r 0.3.0
text: Added CI, README.Rmd, reproducible package logo source cleaner release maintenance. onet_archive_read() now preserves task DWA native fields reading O*NET Task Statements, Task Ratings, Tasks DWAs, 30.3 GWAs IWAs DWAs file. onet_crosswalk_bridge() now keeps 8-digit O*NET-SOC detail codes native bridge grain includes derived 6-digit SOC columns employment joins. onet_decompose_change() decomposes aggregate change within, , interaction, unclassifiable components sum--total check. onet_measure(), onet_task_to_occupation(), onet_measure_aggregate(), onet_robustness_diagnostic() add bring---measure validation, task--occupation aggregation, employment-weighted aggregation, provenance, plumbing-sensitivity checks. onet_panel_reconcile() now classifies transition rows, suppressed rows, new rows, dropped rows explicitly instead counting ordinary matched updates. onet_reference_soc_resolve(), onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums() create reference-SOC mappings normalized employment-weight panels OEWS PUMS workflows.

title: onet2r 0.2.0
text: Added longitudinal O*NET background article verified reference file help users understand archive panels, taxonomy changes, comparability limits interpreting release--release changes. onet_archive_download() downloads text-format O*NET database archives release version reproducible local analysis. onet_archive_read() reads descriptor tables downloaded archives normalized long panel schema now accepts local ZIP files extracted archive directories offline workflows. onet_change_summary() summarizes reconciled O*NET changes overall SOC job family. onet_crosswalk_bridge() builds SOC taxonomy bridges across supported O*NET vintages labels one--one, split, merge mappings. onet_panel() assembles descriptor tables across multiple O*NET releases longitudinal analysis can use local archive paths explicit release dates. onet_panel_reconcile() classifies release--release changes real updates, stable resampling, stale carryforwards, recode/recalculation flags. onet_releases() lists O*NET database releases, archive URLs, dictionary URLs, release dates, inferred SOC taxonomy vintages.

title: onet2r 0.1.0
text: onet_cache_use(), onet_cache_clear(), onet_rate_limit() add optional local response caching request spacing repeated bulk O*NET API workflows. onet_join_oews() joins O*NET occupation tibbles national BLS Occupational Employment Wage Statistics (OEWS) employment wage estimates. onet_oews(), onet_oews_national(), onet_oews_state(), onet_oews_metro(), onet_oews_industry() download parse BLS OEWS estimates SOC-level wage employment context. onet_pums_employment_weights() converts ACS/CPS-style PUMS occupation records SOC-level employment weights O*NET joins. onet_weighted_summary() computes employment- wage-weighted summaries O*NET task, skill, ability, work-activity rows. Initial development release O*NET authentication, occupation search, occupation detail helpers, database table access, pagination, military crosswalks, taxonomy mapping.
